contract.input: contiguous FP16 or BF16 [rows, 2 * width] with split-half gate/up layout
contract.scales: F32 logical [rows, width / group_size], row-major publicly and row/group-major through the vLLM-compatible operator
contract.quantization: symmetric dynamic per-block with qmax 448 and minimum scale 1 / (448 * 512)
contract.arithmetic: SiLU activation and multiplication remain F32 until direct FP8 conversion
contract.stream: caller-selected CUDA stream; PyTorch uses the current stream
contract.aliasing: input, output, and scales must not overlap

implementation.mapping: one CUDA block per flattened row/group pair
implementation.threads: one thread per element; 64 or 128 threads
implementation.fusion: one launch; fused value remains in a register across scale reduction

validation.torch_compile: unchecked and auto-functionalized mutable boundaries passed fullgraph compilation
validation.vllm_fusion_registration: dynamic symmetric FP8 group-64 and group-128 entries mapped to Loom
validation.vllm_pattern_rewrite: official dynamic group-128 activation-quant pattern matched once and emitted Loom as the auto-functionalized mutable target

raw_cuda.protocol: Cargo bench, CUDA events, CPU oracle before timing; 100 warmups, 9 samples, 1000 launches per sample

vllm_named_baseline.protocol: same preallocated PyTorch C++ custom-operator boundary, one second BF16 GEMM warmup per provider, 100 operator warmups, 15 samples, 2000 launches per sample

composed_semantic_difference.reason: standalone vLLM SiLU rounds a BF16 intermediate before per-group quantization

vllm_named_baseline.interpretation: Against the semantically identical vLLM fused operator, Loom's advantage is order-stable: 1.216-1.231x eager speedup ratio (17.7-18.8% lower latency) and 1.037-1.082x CUDA Graph ratio (3.6-7.5% lower latency). The 2.14-2.36x composed-path ratios include both launch fusion and different intermediate-rounding semantics.

engine_gate.compiler_boundary: vLLM 0.24 fusion-table registration, official pattern rewrite, and auto-functionalized torch.compile path passed
engine_gate.interpretation: compiler integration is proven, but a real quantized model must invoke the fusion before any engine or end-to-end claim

claim: Loom implements a Rust/CUDA/PyTorch/vLLM-compatible fused SiLU-and-Mul plus dynamic per-block FP8 operator. H20 evidence closes contract, exact correctness, stream, compilation, CUDA Graph, and named-baseline gates with an order-stable operator-level advantage; real FP8-model and end-to-end gates remain open.
